title: OpenClaw Setup Command and Requirements Research
key_findings: openclaw setup is non-interactive and safe for automation
No API keys required for base setup - OAuth handled separately
Container failing due to missing config file with gateway.mode=local
Two scriptable options: pre-create config file or use --allow-unconfigured flag
Onboard wizard cannot be fully automated due to OAuth flows
Recommended approach: install base system, then prompt user for onboard wizard
Config stored at ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json, workspace at ~/.openclaw/workspace

title: Current OpenClaw Installation State Assessment
key_findings: No OpenClaw container or image exists on this system
Doppler CLI installed but not authenticated with valid token
Project contains only installer scripts and documentation, not a live deployment
Docker daemon running healthy with other unrelated containers
Intended deployment is Proxmox LXC container, not direct host installation

title: OpenClaw LXC Container State Assessment
key_findings: Gateway in crash-restart loop due to missing openclaw.json configuration file
Docker and Doppler properly configured and working
Volume mounts correct: /root/.openclaw mounted to /home/node/.openclaw in container
No network or infrastructure issues - only missing config file
Solution: Run openclaw setup to create configuration

title: OpenClaw Gateway Configuration Fix
key_findings: Gateway requires explicit gateway.mode=local setting
Container runs as uid 1000 requiring read access to config
Canvas and cron directories need proper ownership
Gateway exposes ports 18789 (gateway) and 18790 (bridge)
All services start successfully when config is properly configured

title: OpenClaw Gateway Token Authentication Research
key_findings: Token stored in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json under gateway.auth.token
Token passed via WebSocket connect params or URL query parameter
Token is 48-char hex from crypto.randomBytes(24)
trustedProxies config required for reverse proxy
Three delivery methods: URL param, WebSocket params, manual entry
Current config missing gateway.auth.token causing unauthorized error
Local bypass requires token configured

title: Gateway Token Configuration
key_findings: Token: a7a3d116acb2dc822631d15f8c785fdfb54c8c0c09a8d628
Tokenized URL: https://openclaw.hoskins.fun?token=a7a3d116acb2dc822631d15f8c785fdfb54c8c0c09a8d628
trustedProxies configured for 10.0.10.0/24, 127.0.0.1, and 172.16.0.0/12
Container restarted successfully and is running

title: OpenClaw Proxy Trust and Schema Research
key_findings: No auth bypass mode exists - token required for all connections
WebSocket disconnects due to missing token in connection params
Schema unavailable caused by permission denied on /home/node/.openclaw/devices
gateway.trustedProxies only affects IP resolution, not authentication
Device directory owned by root prevents node user from creating subdirectories

title: OpenClaw Permissions Fix
key_findings: Container runs as uid 1000 but host files were owned by root
Recursive chown to 1000:1000 fixed all permission issues
Required directories (devices, workspace, canvas) created successfully
Container now starts cleanly without EACCES errors
Remaining proxy and token warnings are configuration issues, not permission problems

title: OpenClaw Gateway Authentication Disable Research
key_findings: Gateway authentication CANNOT be disabled for non-loopback binds - hardcoded security requirement
ONLY bypass path is allowTailscale with loopback bind (Tailscale Serve mode) - not applicable to NPM setup
Token required in TWO places: config file (gateway.auth.token) AND environment variable (OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN takes precedence)
Browser NOT persisting token - expects tokenized URL or manual paste in Settings (localStorage)
Solution 1 (recommended): Generate tokenized dashboard URL with embedded token, use as NPM proxy target
Solution 2: Manual token paste in Control UI Settings per browser (one-time setup)
Solution 3: Bind to loopback (127.0.0.1) + NPM as only entry point (defense-in-depth, still requires token)
No config options like auth.enabled=false, noAuth, allowAnonymous exist in codebase

title: WebSocket Disconnect Debug Research
key_findings: Token mismatch caused by device-specific token taking precedence over URL token
NPM proxy IP 10.0.10.8 showing untrusted proxy warning despite trustedProxies config
WebSocket auth flow uses device identity with fallback to shared token
Gateway config has correct token but WebSocket client may be using stale device token
Clearing browser storage should resolve issue by removing stale device tokens

title: Trusted Proxy Config Fix
key_findings: Added 10.0.10.8 to trustedProxies array
Gateway restarted successfully
Config change auto-detected and reloaded
Proxy warnings resolved

title: OpenClaw Pairing Requirement Research
key_findings: Device pairing required after browser storage cleared - 3 pending requests found
Pairing uses Ed25519 public key cryptography with SHA-256 derived device IDs
CLI approval blocked by token mismatch: gateway.remote.token not set in container
Auto-approval only for local/trusted clients - user browser via Cloudflare proxy requires manual approval
Fix: Set gateway.remote.token then run: docker exec openclaw-openclaw-gateway-1 node dist/index.js devices approve <requestId>

title: Device Pairing Approval
key_findings: Config requires both gateway.auth.token and gateway.remote.token
CLI not available - used direct Node.js invocation in container
2 devices approved successfully with operator roles

title: OpenClaw API Key Configuration Research
key_findings: API key exists in Doppler but not in auth-profiles.json
Expected path: /home/node/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json
Auth resolution: profiles > env vars > models.json > error
Manual file creation recommended using Doppler secret
Alternative: Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to docker-compose.yml environment

title: OpenClaw API Key Injection Fix
key_findings: docker-compose.yml missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
Successfully added API key to both openclaw-gateway and openclaw-cli services
Container verified with API key present: sk-ant-api03-lhW...
Container running successfully on ports 18789-18790
